text: In our technique, on the left side of the telescope, a curved DAPRI clasper is introduced by direct access through the fascia and on the right side, a low-profile 5 mm cannula is introduced through which the dissecting instruments can be changed frequently
without causing any injury to the fascia and without having any problems in maintaining the pneumoperitoneum.
On the left side, the DAPRI instrument once placed does not need to be changed.
This is the curved DAPRI grasper for laparoscopic cholecystectomy.
It facilitates retraction of the fundus and body of the gallbladder to expose the Hartmann's pouch and the callot's triangle area.
Local anesthetic is infiltrated into the superior fold of the umbilicus to bring the skin more superficially so that the incision can actually be made inside the umbilical skin.
A semilunar incision of about 15 millimeter is required.
Viris needle is introduced and pneumoperitoneum is created.
11 mm cannula is introduced for a 30 degree telescope.
To the right side, the skin is retracted and a 5 mm cannula is introduced.
This five millimeter cannula is then removed and DAPRI curved grasper is introduced by direct access technique through the facial opening.
The curved grasper requires manipulation for advancement.
And this is the view of the grasper inside the peritoneal cavity.
On the left side of the patient now, a low profile five millimeter cannula is introduced.
and this is the position of the instruments in relation to the telescope.
The left-sided curved grasper does not need to be changed even once.
The omentum is adherent to the liver covering the gallbladder.
A window is found for viewing the gallbladder and the omentum is separated from the liver
Maintaining the hemostasis.
Avoiding any bleeding.
Once the fundus is exposed, it is lifted with a DAPRI grasper and additions to the right of the gallbladder are placed.
The aim is to expose the Hartman's pouch.
Once the Hartman's pouch is reached, the grasper is stepped down on the mid body of the gallbladder so that the Hartman's pouch and cystic duct can be elevated.
Posterior dissection is performed first around the neck of the gallbladder.
The position of the common bile duct is noted.
The gallbladder is freed posteriorly and tear dissection is performed in the callotus triangle to expose the cystic artery.
Space is created between the cystic duct and the cystic artery.
We can see the liver through the window between the cystic duct and the cystic artery.
We can see the junction of the cystic duct, the common bile duct and the position of the duodenum.
The cystic artery,
can either be cauterized using a monopolar or bipolar electrocautery or can alternatively be clipped.
The cystic artery is divided
The cystic duct gets lengthened after dividing the cystic artery and then three clips are applied on the cystic duct, two on the CBD side and one on the specimen side.
The cystic duct is divided.
And then the gallbladder is separated from the liver by dividing the peritoneal attachments.

Though it is slightly difficult to manipulate the gallbladder through a single grasper but with practice and with patience the gallbladder can be effectively manipulated to expose both the medial as well as the lateral side for detachment.
One must stay in the correct plane to avoid any bleeding from the liver bed.
and also not to open up the gallbladder.
After the gallbladder is separated completely from its bed, the liver is lifted up using the grasper and the area is inspected.
The gallbladder fossa is thoroughly washed.
This is the final inspection
of the gallbladder pedicle showing clips on the cystic duct and the cystic artery with perfect hemostasis.
The curved grasper is now removed.
A 10 mm claw forceps is introduced through the 10 mm port for extraction of the gallbladder and the telescope is changed to 5 mm and introduced through the 5 mm canal.
The gallbladder is held at the point of the neck by the claw forceps.
And the gallbladder is extracted through the 10 millimeter port.
The 10 millimeter facial opening is closed using a vicryl suture.
And this shows that the bite has been taken through strong tissues.
The wound after closing the fascia, the skin falls close to it and this is the final cosmetic results.
The incision cannot even be seen.
